Replace.Text: A Terra-style Voronoi map with a larger landmass ("Old World") and a smaller one ("New World"). Suggested map script for "Terra" Start Position options.
A giant Earth map with fixed real-world geography and true-start support.
Greatest Earth Map (YnAMP)[n]Civ5 map by djvandyke
A huge Earth map with fixed real-world geography and true-start support.
A 128x80 Regional Earth map that imports a 1:1 Giant Earth crop, then replaces edge seam columns to preserve no-wrap behavior. Start with the classic Colonization section.
Custom huge map size used by Greatest Earth (104x64).
Massive map size for YnAMP scenarios and scripts.
Giant map size for YnAMP scenarios and scripts.
Ludicrous map size for YnAMP scenarios and scripts.
Region Tagging Mode (for YnAMP TSL maps)

Controls how homeland and distant-lands regions are classified. Landmass uses ocean separation from player starts. Region uses XML-defined superregions on supported Earth maps and falls back to Landmass when XML region data is unavailable.
Homeland and distant lands are determined by ocean-blocked connectivity from player start positions. Isolated islands and ocean remain neutral.
Uses XML-defined superregion data (EurAsia, Africa, Americas, Oceania) for supported Earth maps. Falls back to Landmass if no XML data is available for the selected map.
Europe, the North Atlantic, and the Americas.
Europe, Africa, and Asia on a 128x80 canvas, using a no-wrap Giant Earth crop.
South Asia, Southeast Asia, Oceania, and the Pacific basin on a 128x80 canvas, using a no-wrap Giant Earth crop.
Disable this to remove the impassable terrain on the edges, if you want to be able to circumnavigate the Old World, and reach the East Indies by sailing west.

After placing start positions, shuffles civilizations so that those historically close to each other (using Giant Earth map coordinates as real-world anchors) are placed near each other on the game map. Has no effect on imported Earth maps, which already use true start locations. This will ignore civilization starting biases.

After placing start positions, groups civilizations by continental region: Old World civilizations start on one continent, New World civilizations on the other. Within each continent, culturally adjacent groups are placed near one another. Oceania civilizations spawn on islands in either region. Ignores civilization starting biases. Designed for Terra-style maps where the New World continent is smaller; the number of civilizations per hemisphere will be unbalanced.

First separates civilizations onto hemispheres using continental superregions (Old World / New World), then within each hemisphere shuffles civilizations so that those historically close to each other are placed near each other on the game map. Designed for Terra-style maps where the New World continent is smaller; the number of civilizations per hemisphere will be unbalanced. Ignores civilization starting biases.

Like Cultural Region (Terra), but redistributes culture groups across hemispheres to keep civ counts as equal as possible. All civilizations from the same cultural group always stay on the same hemisphere. Within each hemisphere, culturally adjacent groups are placed near one another. Ignores civilization starting biases.
Percentage of total land area allocated to the smaller landmass ("New World"). The larger landmass (Old World) receives the remainder. Smaller values produce a more extreme size difference.
Controls how civilizations are distributed between the two landmasses. Split places civs on both landmasses proportional to their size. Same Landmass places all civs on the larger landmass.
Controls how much ocean the generator keeps between the Old World and New World landmasses. Wide raises the minimal distance between landmasses. Standard uses the base Continents spacing.
Controls the total amount of land generated. Higher water settings reduce the total land area.
Natural Wonders Placement on TSL Maps

Controls how Natural Wonders are placed on True Start Location Earth maps. "Real World + Random" uses XML real-world positions first, then fills remaining slots randomly. "Real World Only" uses only XML positions (adjacency and elevation requirements are relaxed to improve placement). "Random" ignores all real-world positions and places all wonders randomly.
Regional Resource Placement on TSL Maps
Controls Earth-region resource realism. Curated enforces a curated ruleset for a small set of geographically distinctive resources after normal generation or imported map placement..
No regional cleanup or backfilling is applied. Resources keep the current YnAMP behavior.
Applies a narrow first-pass Earth ruleset for distinctive resources, removing obvious out-of-region placements and guaranteeing a few regional deposits where they are expected.

Controls how geography affects civilization availability on Earth TSL maps. No Unlock keeps placement realism only. Add Unlock keeps the game's normal unlock paths and adds geographic ones. Strict Unlock keeps only geographic unlocks plus explicitly preserved local or adjacent original unlocks.
No geography-based unlock changes are applied. Realistic resource placement can still run independently.
Keeps the game's existing unlock paths and adds geography-based unlocks when the player controls qualifying regional resources.
Limits civilization availability to geography-based unlocks and explicitly preserved nearby original unlocks, removing other non-geographic unlock paths such as leader or reward-driven availability.
Natural Wonders are placed entirely randomly, ignoring real-world positions.
Uses XML real-world positions first, then fills any remaining Natural Wonder slots with random placement.
Only XML real-world positions are used. No random fill. Adjacency and elevation placement requirements are removed from the game database to improve placement success.
Exploration Ocean Travel on Large Maps

Controls deep-ocean movement on Large and larger maps during the Exploration Age. Standard keeps vanilla rules. Relaxed Movement removes the turn-ending ocean obstacle. Relaxed Movement + Reduced Damage also lowers base ocean attrition.
Keeps the vanilla Exploration deep-ocean movement and damage rules.

On Large and larger maps, entering Ocean no longer ends a unit's turn. Cartography grants embarked units +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile, Astronomy grants naval units +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile, and Shipbuilding grants +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile to all units.
Also reduces base deep-ocean damage on Large and larger maps.
All units gain +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile.
Additional +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile.
Naval units gain +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile.
Embarked units gain +1 Movement when starting on an Ocean tile.
